class NestedIterator {
public:
    vector<int> flatList;
    int currentIdx;
    
    NestedIterator(vector<NestedInteger> &nestedList) {
        currentIdx = 0;
        for (auto ni : nestedList) {
            flatten(ni);
        }
    }
    
    void flatten(NestedInteger ni) {
        if (ni.isInteger()) {
            flatList.push_back(ni.getInteger());
        } else {
            vector<NestedInteger> &list = ni.getList();
            for (auto next : list) {
                flatten(next);
            }
        }
    }
    
    int next() {
        return flatList[currentIdx++];
    }
    
    bool hasNext() {
        return currentIdx < flatList.size();
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
class NestedIterator {
public:
    vector<int> data;
    int idx;
    
    NestedIterator(vector<NestedInteger> &nestedList) {
        idx = 0;
        for (auto& ni : nestedList) {
            flatten(ni);
        }
    }
    
    void flatten(NestedInteger ni) {
        if (ni.isInteger()) {
            data.push_back(ni.getInteger());
        } else {
            auto list = ni.getList();
            for (auto& nextNi : list) {
                flatten(nextNi);
            }
        }
    }
    
    int next() {
        return data[idx++];
    }
    
    bool hasNext() {
        return idx < data.size();
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n) for constructor, O(1) for next and hasNext
space complexity: O(n) where n is total integers
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution pre-flattens all integers into a vector during initialization using depth-first traversal. This allows O(1) time for next() and hasNext(). The main weakness is upfront O(n) processing and O(n) space, but this ensures optimal performance for iteration operations.
